message:
fix(events): bump sent_seq before broadcast_send to avoid transient queued_len=0

EventBus lag accounting previously ordered operations as:
  broadcast_send(...)
  record_timestamp(...)
  tracker.record_send()  <-- sent_seq incremented LAST

Subscribers consume via fetch_add on their own position counter and then
compute queued depth as `sent_seq.saturating_sub(position)`. Because
broadcast_send wakes receivers before record_send ran, a subscriber could
race: fetch_add its position to N+1 while sent_seq still read N. The
saturating_sub then clamped to 0, causing queued_len to transiently
report "nothing queued" in the exact window where we just enqueued.

Reorder: record_send() BEFORE broadcast_send. Now sent_seq always leads
(or equals) subscriber positions:
  - Send succeeds, subscriber exists  → sent_seq already incremented,
    queued_len reports ≥1 correctly.
  - Send succeeds, no subscribers     → sent_seq over-counts by 1, but
    positions is empty so queued_len returns 0 anyway; harmless.
  - Send fails (no receivers)         → same harmless over-count; the
    error branch returns 0 count regardless.

Added inline comment explaining the ordering invariant so future
refactors don't reintroduce the race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
